Scenario.title: Complex KV Botnet Activity Scenario
Scenario.overview: Transportation Systems Complex Expert attackers from the KV Botnet group target transportation systems, exploiting vulnerabilities in public-facing applications to gain initial access.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Device theft, Weak passwords, Improper configuration, Vulnerability, Internet-facing assets, Shared DB or directory, Obsolete products, Injection flaws, Custom APIs, Autonomous system numbers(ASNs), SSL Certificates and Attribution, WHOIS records, contacts and history, TCP/IP ports, Public and private cloud, Web server services such as emails and databases, Shadow IT, Poor implementation of access controls, Malicious insiders, Social engineering, Insufficient physical security controls, Authentication issues
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: External/Removable media, Loss or Theft of equipment, Attrition, Web, Account takeover, Inadequate encryption, Browser-based attack, Application compromise, Open port, Vulnerability exploit, Ransomware, Malware, Man in The Middle(MiTM), Session hijacking, Brute-force attack, Distributed Denial of Service(DDoS), Watering-hole, Compromised and weak credentials, SIM Swap, Security misconfigurations and vulnerabilities, Web forms, IP address and IP blocks, Domains and subdomains, Botnet, Credential compromise, Telephony Denial of Service(TDoS), Replay Attack, Cryptanalysis Attacks, Side Channel Attacks, Hardware Trojans, Covert Attacks, Sleep Deprivation Attack, Clock Skewing, Attacks Targeting System Reputation, Data and Command Integrity Attack, Eavesdropping, Command and Control Attacks, Phishing, Impersonations/Spoofing, Improper usage, Dumpster diving, Advanced Persistent Threat(APT), Baiting, Shoulder surfing, Reverse social engineering, Business Email Compromise(BEC), Advanced Fee, Extortion

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: The attackers exploit a vulnerability in a public-facing application to gain initial access to the targeted transportation system.

stage.name: Execution
stage.description: The attackers establish command and control channels over the internet to maintain persistence and execute further attacks.

stage.name: Persistence
stage.description: The attackers modify the system image to persist their presence and evade detection.

stage.name: Privilege Escalation
stage.description: The attackers escalate privileges to gain higher-level access to the system.

stage.name: Defense Evasion
stage.description: The attackers employ anti-analysis techniques to evade detection and hide their tracks.

stage.name: Credential Access
stage.description: The attackers attempt to brute-force login credentials to gain unauthorized access.

stage.name: Discovery
stage.description: The attackers scan the network to identify potential targets and gather information.

stage.name: Lateral Movement
stage.description: The attackers move laterally across the network to expand their reach.

stage.name: Collection
stage.description: The attackers collect sensitive data from the local system.

stage.name: Exfiltration
stage.description: The attackers exfiltrate stolen data over alternative protocols.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: The attackers launch a denial-of-service attack to disrupt the system.

Scenario.summary: The KV Botnet group employs complex tactics to breach transportation systems, starting with exploiting public-facing applications, establishing command and control channels, modifying system images, escalating privileges, evading defenses, accessing credentials, gathering information, moving laterally, collecting data, exfiltrating stolen data, and finally launching a denial-of-service attack.
